scenario: A complex Form has 30 fields. The team wants to group related fields visually -- Personal Info, Contact, Preferences, Consents -- with sub-sections of the form clearly separated.
question: Which Form component supports nested layout / grouping of fields?

options.text: Group component -- visually groups child fields with an optional label or heading, rendering as a distinct section. It provides nesting: a Group can contain any other form components including Text Inputs, Datepickers, Dropdowns, and even other Groups for deep nesting. Combined with Heading and Text View components for section titles and descriptions, the Group component organises complex forms with many fields into readable, logically separated sections.

Use the Section component to visually separate related fields into distinct sections -- some Web Modeler versions may include specific section-style components. Verify the specific section components available in your version's element library before designing around them.

A single Form with Group components achieves the same visual separation of Personal Info, Contact, Preferences, and Consents sections without requiring multiple form definitions, multiple User Task configurations, or a multi-step approval flow. The Group component exists specifically to solve this in-form layout problem.

The Camunda Forms component library does not include any layout grouping component for organising related fields -- this misrepresents the available form components. The form-js library and Web Modeler Forms element library include layout components designed for grouping, including the Group component with optional labels and support for nested component hierarchies. These enable structured, readable forms for complex data collection scenarios with many fields.

a.text: Correct. Camunda Forms' element library typically includes a Group component for nested layout / clustering: Visual grouping: child fields are rendered in a visually-distinct group (often with a label / heading). Nesting: Group can contain any other Form components, including other Groups (deep nesting if needed). Optional title / label: provides section heading. Combined with other layout components: Heading / Text View: section titles, instructions, help text. Spacer: vertical / horizontal spacing. Group: cluster related fields. Example structure for a complex form: ` Heading: "Customer Registration" Group: "Personal Info" Text Input: First Name Text Input: Last Name Datepicker: Date of Birth Group: "Contact" Text Input: Email Text Input: Phone Group: "Preferences" Checklist: Communication channels Group: "Consents" Checkbox: Privacy Policy Checkbox: Marketing ` Renders as a structured form with clear sections; readers see the logical grouping. Verify the specific Group / Section components in your Web Modeler version's element library.

b.text: Incorrect. Section may exist; Group is the canonical mechanism in many versions.
